name: Chinese: Barbecued Pork Bun (Cha Siu Bow)
recipeInstructions:
--------------------------
Filling:
6 oz Chinese BBQ pork, diced
1 tablespoon Oil
2 teaspoons Water
1/2 teaspoon Salt
1/2 teaspoon Sugar
1/2 teaspoon Thin soy sauce
1 teaspoon Oyster sauce
1 teaspoon Hoisin sauce
2 teaspoons Cornstarch
4 tablespoons Cold water (For thickening)
Mix together the warm water, 1/2 tsp. sugar and yeast in an 8 oz. measuring cup. Let stand until it reaches the 8 oz. level (about 20 minutes).
Sift flour, cake flour, sugar and salt into a large mixing bowl.
Add shortening, yeast mixture and milk.
Knead mixture 5 minutes to form a dough. Cover with a damp cloth and set dough in a warm place. Allow the dough to rise for 3 hours.
Heat wok, add oil and stir-fry pork for 2 minutes. Add 1 tablespoon water, salt, sugar, soy sauce, oyster sauce and hoisin sauce. Bring it to a boil.
Prepare thickening by mixing cornstarch and 4 tablespoons cold water. Stir into the mixture and cook for 1 minute. Let cool before using.
After 3 hours, when the dough has risen, shape into rolls about 2 inches in diameter. Cut each roll into 1-1/2 inch pieces.
Shape each piece into a shallow bowl shape. Place 1 tablespoon filling in the center, close and twist dough to form a bun. Put the bun on a 2-inch square of white paper. (This prevents the bun from becoming soggy while steaming.) Place 8 buns in a pie pan and allow them to rest for 15 minutes in a warm place.
Steam for 25 minutes.
